pi_items.source: Checked verifier.py logic where `abs(rep_x_main - expected_x_main) <= tol` uses 0.15
Checked export_result.sh and verifier.py where RESULT_FILE is defined.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires mathematically computing the main and nose gear X-coordinates for a NASA eCRM-001 aircraft model based on synthetic tip-back and static load constraints injected via setup_task.sh. Independent trigonometric calculations confirm that a 15-degree tip-back angle at a Max Aft CG of 35.0 m and a vertical height of 4.0 m requires a main gear X-coordinate of 36.07. Furthermore, carrying 10% static load at a Fwd CG of 32.5 m requires the nose gear to be at exactly 0.35. Both expected values explicitly match the mathematical truth and verifier logic. All metadata parameters and output paths are verified.
